Pterocarpus grandiflorus, commonly known as the "Gothofreda" or "Brazilian Pterocarpus", is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterocarpus grandiflorus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
